channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Detroit to Join PWHL as Ninth Team for Upcoming Season
item.description: The Premier Women's Hockey League (PWHL) has announced Detroit as the location for its ninth team, set to start competing next season. This expansion marks the league’s third new team since it began i...

item.title: Digitizing the Photographic Legacy of Vitaliy Kuzovkov: Kyiv in the 1980s
item.description: Darka Simchich, a 23-year-old educational manager at Kyiv-Mohyla Business School, recently began digitizing the film archive of her great-grandfather, photographer Vitaliy Kuzovkov. His vibrant and wa...

item.title: Mike Cannon-Brookes leads Atlassian solo amid AI-driven product expansion and workforce changes
item.description: Mike Cannon-Brookes, co-founder of Atlassian, is navigating the company solo for nearly two years after Scott Farquhar stepped back. The Sydney-based software giant recently faced a 70% drop in share ...

item.title: Boy in Odisha Trapped in Crevice Encounters Snake
item.description: In a startling incident from Odisha, a young boy got his head stuck in a narrow crevice while playing near his home. As he struggled to free himself, he noticed a snake staring directly at him, which ...

item.title: Philippines Hosts 48th ASEAN Summit in Cebu to Strengthen Regional Cooperation
item.description: The Philippines is currently hosting the 48th ASEAN Summit in Lapu-Lapu City, Cebu, marking the country’s fifth time to chair the regional bloc. ASEAN, or the Association of Southeast Asian Nations, w...

item.title: CNN Founder Ted Turner Dies at 87 After Battle With Lewy Body Dementia
item.description: Ted Turner, the American media mogul wey found CNN, don die at the age of 87. CNN confirm the news, noting say Turner had been battling Lewy body dementia, one degenerative brain disease. Turner na on...

item.title: Northern Ontario communities face rising overdose deaths and urgent need for addiction support
item.description: Northern Ontario is experiencing a sharp rise in overdose deaths, prompting health experts, front-line workers, and addiction specialists to call for expanded treatment options. Public Health Sudbury ...

item.title: Exploring the Thames: The History and Hobby of Mudlarking
item.description: Mudlarking is a unique practice rooted in the history of London, where enthusiasts search the exposed banks of the Thames for artifacts and historical objects. Originating in the 18th century as a way...

item.title: Congress Suffers Major Defeat in Assam Assembly Elections, BJP Consolidates Power
item.description: In the 2026 Assam Assembly elections, Congress faced a significant setback, losing its traditional strongholds and failing to convert the popularity of its chief ministerial candidate, Gaurav Gogoi, i...

item.title: Driver avoids prison despite fleeing fatal e-scooter accident
item.description: A driver involved in a fatal e-scooter accident has been sentenced without receiving jail time, despite failing to stop at the scene of the crash. The incident has raised concerns about road safety an...

item.title: Canadian privacy regulators find gaps in OpenAI’s data collection and transparency practices
item.description: Canadian federal and provincial privacy regulators have concluded that OpenAI did not comply with national privacy laws when collecting data to train its ChatGPT system. The findings come from a joint...

item.title: JUI-F stages protests in KP following targeted killing of Maulana Idrees
item.description: On Wednesday, Jamiat Ulema-i-Islam-Fazl (JUI-F) organised widespread protest rallies across Khyber Pakhtunkhwa in response to the killing of prominent cleric and former MPA Maulana Muhammad Idrees. Th...

item.title: Nigeria Must Protect Press Freedom to Strengthen Democracy
item.description: Nigeria dey for one critical moment as the world celebrate World Press Freedom Day (WPFD) on May 3. This day na reminder say free press na cornerstone for democracy, good governance, and accountabilit...

item.title: Bustos Dam Irrigation Supply Extended to Support Central Luzon Farms
item.description: The National Water Resources Board (NWRB) has approved the extension of irrigation supply from Angat Dam to Bustos Dam to support farmlands in Bulacan and Pampanga. Originally scheduled allocations ex...

item.title: Primm Valley Casino Resort to Close, Ending Era of Full-Time Gambling in Primm
item.description: Primm Valley Casino Resorts, the last full-time casino in Primm, Nevada, is set to close permanently, with employee terminations scheduled by July 4, 2026. The closure marks the end of a gambling era ...

item.title: New Political Party Gaza Democratic Front Focuses on Service Delivery in Limpopo and Beyond
item.description: A newly formed political party called the Gaza Democratic Front (GDF) is making its mark in South African local politics, particularly in Limpopo. Led by Reason Mthombeni, the party aims to address ev...

item.title: Federal Government Approves Reconstruction of Century-Old Carter Bridge in Lagos
item.description: The Federal Government don begin work to reconstruct the Carter Bridge, wey don dey for Lagos for almost 100 years. Dem approve a total of ₦548.98 billion to demolish the old bridge wey get serious da...

item.title: Flooding disrupts schools, businesses and emergency response in Nelson Mandela Bay
item.description: Heavy rainfall in Nelson Mandela Bay led to widespread flooding on Wednesday, forcing schools to close and disrupting business operations across the metro. While some larger manufacturers managed to c...

item.title: Honda cancels Ontario EV plant amid shifting market demand and U.S. tariffs
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney acknowledged that Canada’s auto sector is encountering significant challenges after Honda confirmed it would withdraw from a planned $15-billion electric vehicle (EV) plant ...

item.title: President Ruto Receives New Ambassadors, Calls for Closer Diplomatic and Economic Ties
item.description: On Wednesday, President William Ruto officially received Letters of Credence from three newly appointed envoys to Kenya at a ceremony held at State House, Nairobi. The diplomats presented included Amb...
